title: Add Mesh Validation Commands to Automation CLI
description: Extend the automation API and Python CLI with mesh-focused validation helpers on top of the new mesh viewer/runtime path. The goal is to make procedural mesh work easier to validate without hand-inspecting the full generic ui-state payload or manually chaining health, mesh-state, and screenshot commands. Focus on agent-friendly commands like mesh validation summaries and mesh-specific probe flows that can be reused by later mesh node tickets.
definition-of-done: 1. The automation stack exposes mesh-focused validation data beyond the minimal vertex/face counts currently available. 2. The Python CLI has a mesh validation command that returns machine-readable mesh health information and a non-zero exit code on failure. 3. The Python CLI has a mesh-focused probe command that bundles the common health/state/screenshot flow for mesh scenes. 4. Command help stays registry-driven and machine-readable for agent use. 5. At least one automated validation path is exercised against the mesh viewer scene. 6. Existing CLI commands remain backward compatible.
testing-plan: 1. Run Python CLI unit tests covering the new mesh commands. 2. Run targeted Java tests for any new scene/runtime automation-visible mesh state. 3. Launch mesh-viewer and run health, mesh-state, mesh-validate, and mesh-probe end to end. 4. Confirm mesh-validate exits successfully for the default cube mesh and returns stable JSON. 5. Confirm mesh-probe captures a screenshot and includes mesh-specific summary data.
todos: DONE : Expose richer mesh validation fields through the automation-visible mesh scene state
DONE : Add a mesh-validate CLI command with clear machine-readable pass/fail semantics
DONE : Add a mesh-probe CLI command for the common health + mesh-state + screenshot flow
DONE : Add Python CLI tests for the new mesh commands
DONE : Add or update Java tests covering the underlying mesh scene/runtime state
DONE : Validate the new commands against a live mesh-viewer run
unknowns: Whether mesh validation should remain derived from ui-state or get its own dedicated automation endpoint later
How much structural mesh analysis belongs in the CLI versus the Java automation runtime
Whether future mesh commands should load alternate built-in shapes or graph files directly
changes-made: Extended the mesh viewer automation payload with edge count, boundary edge count, degenerate face count, Euler characteristic, closed/open status, radius, and center.
Added mesh-validate and mesh-probe as registry-driven automation CLI commands on top of the existing mesh-state helper.
Added Python CLI test coverage for mesh-state, mesh-validate success/failure, and mesh-probe.
Expanded the mesh viewer lifecycle test to assert the new automation-visible mesh validation fields.
Validated the commands against a live mesh-viewer run, including screenshot capture.
